patch.value: pm install writes absolute-home-path into tracked .managed-extensions.json

When `pm install calendar` runs, the project-scope managed-extensions.json gets `source.input/location` absolute paths like ~/<repo>/packages/pm-calendar. These leak local home paths into a tracked file and break secret scans. Built-in packages bundled with pm-cli should be persisted by package-name reference (or repo-relative path), not absolute filesystem location.

pm install <builtin-name> persists source as {kind: 'builtin', name: ...} or relative-to-repo path; secret scan no longer flags managed-extensions.json after install; teams can share extension setup via committed tracker.

value.note: builtin package install source persisted without local absolute paths
regression for portable builtin managed source records
